methods.description: Anoxic incubations were used to determine CO2 and methane production rates in sediment samples. This method involves preparing sediment samples in a controlled anoxic environment, incubating them, and measuring gas concentrations to study microbial activity and gas production.

steps.description: Triplicates of 20 g of sediment for each sample were placed in 120-mL bottles and closed with butyl rubber stoppers.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment samples in bottles for incubation

steps.description: Samples were handled under a constant flow of molecular nitrogen to minimize oxygen exposure.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain anoxic conditions using molecular nitrogen

steps.description: Each of the triplicates was amended with 5 mL oxygen free water and the headspace was repeatedly exchanged with pure nitrogen.
steps.searchable_summary: add oxygen-free water and exchange headspace with nitrogen
outputs: anoxic sediment samples with nitrogen headspace

steps.description: Molecular nitrogen was added to establish a slight overpressure inside each bottle.
steps.searchable_summary: establish overpressure with molecular nitrogen

steps.description: Samples were incubated in the dark at 4°C for 2501 days (Kurungnakh Island) and for up to 1229 days (Bol'shoy Lyakhovsky Island).
steps.searchable_summary: incubate samples in the dark at 4°C

steps.description: Carbon dioxide and methane concentrations in the headspace were measured with a gas chromatograph.
steps.searchable_summary: measure CO2 and methane concentrations using gas chromatograph

steps.description: The amount of gas in the headspace was calculated based on headspace volume, incubation temperature and pressure inside the bottle using the ideal gas law.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate gas amount using ideal gas law

steps.description: The amount of gas dissolved in water was calculated based on the gas concentration in the headspace, pressure inside the bottle, water content, pH and gas solubility.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate dissolved gas amount using solubility and pH

methods.description: This method involves processing sequencing data to analyze microbial composition. It includes demultiplexing, quality filtering, sequence inference, and taxonomy assignment to study microbial diversity and abundance.

steps.description: Libraries were demultiplexed using Cutadapt where primer sequences had a maximum error rate of 10% and barcodes were required to have a phred score higher than Q25 without any mismatches.
steps.searchable_summary: demultiplex libraries using Cutadapt with error rate and phred score criteria

steps.description: Samples were further processed using the DADA2 pipeline. Read sequences were truncated (250/200 forward/reverse) and quality-filtered before the error model was generated.
steps.searchable_summary: process samples using DADA2 pipeline with truncation and quality filtering

steps.description: After dereplication, sample inference and merging of the paired-end reads, reads are required to have a minimum length of 200 bp.
steps.searchable_summary: dereplicate, infer samples, and merge reads with minimum length requirement

steps.description: The orientation of the reads was standardized by calculating the hamming distances of the sequences and their reverse complement.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize read orientation using hamming distances

steps.description: The sequence table has been created and chimeras were removed using a de novo approach.
steps.searchable_summary: create sequence table and remove chimeras de novo

steps.description: The amplicon sequence variants (ASV) were assigned to the SILVA taxonomy database using vsearch.
steps.searchable_summary: assign ASVs to SILVA taxonomy using vsearch

steps.description: Relative abundances are used for the visualization and comparison of the microbial composition of the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize microbial composition using relative abundances

methods.description: This method describes the process of obtaining and handling permafrost core samples from drilling campaigns in Northeast Siberia, ensuring minimal contamination and proper storage for further analysis.

steps.description: Samples obtained during two drilling campaigns in Northeast Siberia: one on Kurungnakh Island and the second on Bol'shoy Lyakhovsky Island.
steps.searchable_summary: collect permafrost samples from drilling campaigns

steps.description: Drilling was performed without drilling fluids to avoid microbiological contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: perform drilling without fluids to prevent contamination

steps.description: Core segments were placed into sterile plastic bags immediately after removal and stored at about −8°C.
steps.searchable_summary: store core segments in sterile bags at −8°C

steps.description: Cores were transported in frozen conditions in insulated containers with cool packs to Potsdam, Germany.
steps.searchable_summary: transport cores in frozen conditions to Germany

steps.description: Core segments were split along their axis into halves using sterile sampling devices, with a diamond saw in an ice laboratory at −10°C.
steps.searchable_summary: split core segments using diamond saw in ice lab

steps.description: During subsampling, the outer core layer (1-2 cm) was removed to eliminate potentially contaminated outer soil layers.
steps.searchable_summary: remove outer core layer to prevent contamination
